title: C Math Functions
content:
### Math Functions in C

The `<math.h>` header provides mathematical functions.

### Common Math Functions

| Function | Description | Example |
|----------|-------------|---------|
| `sqrt(x)` | Square root | `sqrt(16.0)` returns 4.0 |
| `pow(x, y)` | x raised to power y | `pow(2.0, 3.0)` returns 8.0 |
| `abs(x)` | Absolute value (int) | `abs(-5)` returns 5 |
| `fabs(x)` | Absolute value (double) | `fabs(-3.14)` returns 3.14 |
| `ceil(x)` | Rounds up | `ceil(3.2)` returns 4.0 |
| `floor(x)` | Rounds down | `floor(3.8)` returns 3.0 |
| `round(x)` | Rounds to nearest | `round(3.5)` returns 4.0 |
| `sin(x)` | Sine (radians) | `sin(0.0)` returns 0.0 |
| `cos(x)` | Cosine (radians) | `cos(0.0)` returns 1.0 |
| `tan(x)` | Tangent (radians) | `tan(0.0)` returns 0.0 |
| `log(x)` | Natural logarithm | `log(2.71828)` ~ 1.0 |
| `log10(x)` | Base-10 logarithm | `log10(100.0)` returns 2.0 |
| `exp(x)` | e raised to power x | `exp(1.0)` returns e |
| `fmod(x, y)` | Floating-point remainder | `fmod(5.5, 2.0)` returns 1.5 |

### Compilation Note

On many systems, you must link the math library with `-lm`:

```
gcc program.c -o program -lm
```

---

> **Key Point:** Link the math library with `-lm` flag during compilation. Math functions typically work with `double` type.

quiz.question: What compiler flag is often needed to link math functions?
